methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Poolside is a Series B artificial intelligence and software company founded by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant. The company has raised $626M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—alongside one public reference point (Palantir). The wide dispersion in this peer set ($500M to $32.0B) reflects substantial heterogeneity in AI & Software Series B valuations.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $626M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the five comparables produces a $5.0B implied valuation, though the median masks significant scatter: the interquartile range spans from $1.0B to $5.6B. 

The combined analysis supports a range of $1.0B (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $3.1B. The principal uncertainty is stage-specific positioning within the peer set. Poolside's capital efficiency relative to peers and competitive positioning within frontier AI would materially influence placement within this range. The outsized valuation of Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B) represents an outlier; excluding it would narrow the range substantially but remains a legitimate comparable at equivalent stage.
